evidence: src/swiss_statistics_mcp/server.py: 9 tools, all snake_case with bfs_ prefix, no spaces/dots/specials
All 9 tools have docstrings 393-930 chars with use-case context
9 distinct tools covering catalog, search, metadata, data, convenience, comparison — no functional duplication
Every @mcp.tool has a multi-paragraph docstring with parameter examples and next_step hints (server.py:469-1325)
Pydantic BaseModel with ConfigDict(extra='forbid') on all 9 tool inputs (server.py:173-345)
Strong constraints: pattern='^(de|fr|it|en)$', ge=1/le=5000, min/max_length
Outputs are JSON strings via json.dumps (return type: str)
No structured output schema / typed return models
gaps: Tools return JSON-encoded strings rather than structured Pydantic outputs; clients must re-parse

evidence: Errors return localized JSON via _format_error() with hint field (server.py:119)
404 distinguishes 'table not found' from generic 500 (server.py:785)
next_step hints returned in tool responses guide LLM to follow-up tools (e.g. bfs_list_tables_by_theme → bfs_get_table_metadata)
All tools are read-only GET/POST against public BFS API — idempotent by construction
max_rows safety limit (default 500, max 5000) — but truncation only signaled via German prose in 'note' field
gaps: No machine-readable 'truncated: true' boolean; LLM must parse text to know data was capped

evidence: src/ layout, pyproject.toml, tests/, .github/workflows/, LICENSE (MIT), README.md + README.de.md, CHANGELOG.md
Demo SVG under docs/assets/, README has Architecture section, anchor demo query documented
from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP (server.py:20)
mcp[cli]>=1.0.0 in pyproject.toml
pydantic>=2.0.0, BaseModel with model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid') on every input model
Async/await consistently used: 26 occurrences, all I/O via httpx.AsyncClient
server.py:1352 starts streamable-http on port 8000 with no host binding, no Origin check, no auth
README recommends public Render.com deployment without front-door auth
gaps: Streamable-HTTP transport on a publicly reachable URL with no Origin validation enables DNS-rebinding attacks and lets any web origin proxy tools through the deployed server

evidence: grep -rE 'os.environ|getenv|API_KEY|SECRET|TOKEN' src/ → no matches (no secrets handled)
No exec/eval/subprocess/os.system/shell=True anywhere in src/
All user input flows through Pydantic with strict regex patterns (lang, theme_code, region) and bounded numerics (ge/le)
URL construction uses f-strings with values already validated by Pydantic regex (pattern='^\d{2}$' for theme codes, '^(de|fr|it|en)$' for lang)
table_id field has min_length=5 but no regex pattern restricting characters
table_id is interpolated into URL path: f'{BFS_API_BASE}/{lang}/{dbid}/{dbid}.px' (server.py:112)
gaps: A malicious table_id like '../../etc/passwd' would be URL-encoded by httpx but no defense-in-depth allowlist regex (e.g. ^[a-z0-9_-]+$) is enforced

evidence: httpx.AsyncClient default verifies TLS; BFS endpoint is HTTPS-only (https://www.pxweb.bfs.admin.ch)
No file-system writes, no temp file creation, no upload handling in src/
No logging configured at all — zero `import logging`, no logger calls
No audit trail for who called which tool with what params
gaps: When deployed to Render.com, an operator has zero visibility into tool invocations, latency, or anomalies

evidence: No PII in responses by design (statistical aggregates only); no PII logged (because nothing is logged)
grep for hardcoded API-key patterns → no matches; only public BFS_API_BASE URL
httpx timeout=30s is set, but no rate limiting per-tool
No retry/backoff for transient 5xx errors
bfs_compare_cantons can fan out N sequential requests (one per canton) with no concurrency cap
gaps: A hostile/buggy client can DoS the upstream BFS API through the server; no internal request budget per call

evidence: URL host is hard-coded BFS_API_BASE; user input only fills path segments validated by regex/length
No URL fragment is user-provided as full URL
No CORS / headers handling needed for stdio; streamable-http relies on FastMCP defaults
Pydantic input validation rejects malformed payloads before tool body executes
except Exception as e: return _format_error(f'Unerwarteter Fehler: {type(e).__name__}: {e}') in 8 places (server.py:520, 613, 694, 793, 878, 1046, 1170, 1260)
gaps: Raw exception message leaked to client — could include internal paths, library traces, or upstream payload details. Should log full error server-side, return sanitized message to client.

evidence: httpx timeout=30s (HTTP_TIMEOUT, server.py:29) on every outbound request
grep for retry|backoff|tenacity → 0 matches
gaps: Transient upstream 503/504 from BFS API surfaces directly as user-facing 'API-Fehler'; even one retry with exponential backoff would absorb >90% of flaps

evidence: Catalog cache implemented with TTL (CATALOG_CACHE_TTL, server.py:73-163)
gaps: No cache for table metadata or data responses — every bfs_get_table_metadata call hits BFS, even for unchanged tables. Metadata is highly cacheable.

evidence: bfs_compare_cantons iterates cantons sequentially (no asyncio.gather)
gaps: For N cantons the latency is N × ~1s instead of ~1s. asyncio.gather with a Semaphore(5) would parallelize safely.

evidence: max_rows hard cap of 5000 prevents unbounded responses; default 500
grep 'import logging' src/ → 0 matches
No print(), no logger anywhere
gaps: Zero observability — operator cannot tell if tools succeed, fail, or are even being called

evidence: No structured (JSON) logging configured
No request_id / correlation_id propagation
gaps: Cloud deployment on Render.com will receive stderr noise from httpx only; cannot correlate a user complaint to a specific call

evidence: No metrics emitted (Prometheus / OpenTelemetry / StatsD)
gaps: No way to track p95 latency, error rates, or per-tool usage

evidence: Try/except patterns differentiate httpx.HTTPStatusError vs generic Exception — good
gaps: The catch-all `except Exception as e` swallows e.g. JSON parse errors and surfaces them as generic 'Fehler' without server-side trace

evidence: README documents 30s timeout, cache TTL, and known limitations explicitly
43 unit tests in tests/test_server.py across 13 test classes; CI on Python 3.11/3.12/3.13
gaps: No security scan in CI (bandit, pip-audit, semgrep) — pyproject has only ruff + pytest in [dev]

evidence: README.md + README.de.md cover install/quickstart/tools/architecture/limitations/deployment
CHANGELOG.md present
pyproject classifier: 'Development Status :: 3 - Alpha', version 0.1.0
gaps: No documented phased rollout / deprecation policy; no versioning policy stated; cloud deployment instructions exist without 'beta' caveats

evidence: README explicitly cites BFS OGD licence with link to nutzungsbedingungen.html
data source documented as 'Open Government Data (OGD) — BFS Terms of Use'
